seo.title: AG2 Guardrails for Healthcare
seo.description: Compliance guide for AG2 agents in healthcare. Meet HIPAA, FDA 21 CFR Part 11 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Healthcare teams using AG2 for multi-agent conversations face heightened risks due to the handling of sensitive patient data and critical decision-making processes. Without robust compliance guardrails, agents can spiral into off-topic discussions, propagate incorrect medical facts, or generate harmful code outputs, violating HIPAA and risking patient harm. Vex provides essential oversight by registering as a message hook to enforce auditable, secure interactions in AG2 group chats.

complianceRequirements.description: Protects PHI from unauthorized disclosure; patient data cannot leave the processing environment and must be de-identified before LLM processing.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex scans AG2 messages for PHI patterns, enforces de-identification via hooks, and blocks transmission outside compliant environments.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires electronic records and signatures to be trustworthy, reliable, and auditable for AI-assisted medical decisions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex logs all agent interactions with timestamps and attributions, ensuring full audit trails for regulatory validation.

complianceRequirements.description: Strengthens HIPAA with breach notifications and higher penalties for mishandling PHI in digital health systems.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex detects potential breaches in real-time during AG2 conversations and triggers automated alerts for immediate response.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates controls for security, availability, and confidentiality in healthcare AI platforms over extended periods.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex continuously monitors AG2 group chats for security violations, ensuring sustained compliance through runtime enforcement.

industryRisks.name: Agent conversation spiraling off-topic
industryRisks.description: Multi-agent discussions deviate from clinical protocols, leading to unverified treatment recommendations.
industryRisks.example: AG2 group chat shifts from diabetes management to unrelated diet fads, recommending unsafe insulin adjustments.

industryRisks.name: Group chat consensus on incorrect facts
industryRisks.description: Agents collectively affirm hallucinated medical data, influencing clinician decisions.
industryRisks.example: Agents agree on fictitious drug interaction, causing contraindicated prescription.

industryRisks.name: Code execution agents producing harmful outputs
industryRisks.description: Agents generate executable code that mishandles patient data or simulates unsafe procedures.
industryRisks.example: Code agent outputs script exposing PHI to external APIs, violating HIPAA.

industryRisks.name: Speaker selection bias in multi-agent groups
industryRisks.description: Biased turn-taking favors non-expert agents, skewing consensus on critical health decisions.
industryRisks.example: Junior diagnostic agent dominates over specialist, misdiagnosing rare condition.

implementation.title: Register Vex Message Hook for PHI Detection
implementation.description: Install Vex and hook into AG2 agents to scan for PHI before processing, enforcing de-identification.
implementation.code:
import vex
import ag2

# Install: pip install ag2 vex-sdk
vex.api_key = "your-vex-key"

@vex.hook
def check_phi(message: ag2.Message) -> bool:
    if vex.classify(message.content, "phi-detection") == "detected":
        message.content = vex.deidentify(message.content)
    return True

# Register on AG2 group chat
chat = ag2.GroupChat(agents=[doctor_agent, nurse_agent])
chat.register_hook(check_phi)
chat.initiate()

implementation.title: Audit Trail for AI Decisions
implementation.description: Capture all AG2 messages with attributions for FDA 21 CFR Part 11 compliance.
implementation.code:
import vex
import ag2

@vex.hook
def audit_decision(message: ag2.Message) -> bool:
    audit_log = {
        "agent": message.speaker,
        "content": message.content,
        "timestamp": vex.timestamp(),
        "decision_type": vex.classify(message.content, "medical-decision")
    }
    vex.log(audit_log, policy="fda-part11")
    return True

# Apply to healthcare workflow
group = ag2.GroupChat(agents=[agents])
group.register_hook(audit_decision)
response = group.run("Analyze patient symptoms")

implementation.title: Prevent Off-Topic Spirals and Bias
implementation.description: Enforce topic adherence and fair speaker selection in AG2 group chats.
implementation.code:
import vex
import ag2

@vex.hook
def enforce_protocol(message: ag2.Message, chat: ag2.GroupChat) -> bool:
    topic_drift = vex.classify(message.content, "off-topic-health")
    if topic_drift.score > 0.7:
        raise ValueError("Conversation off-topic from clinical protocol")
    
    bias_check = vex.analyze(chat.speaker_history, "selection-bias")
    if bias_check.dominant_speaker > 0.6:
        chat.force_next_speaker("specialist_agent")
    return True

workflow = ag2.GroupChat()
workflow.register_hook(enforce_protocol)
workflow.initiate(topic="Patient diagnosis")

caseStudyScenario.heading: AG2 in Healthcare: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
A hospital deploys AG2 for multi-agent triage in the ER, with agents including a diagnostic specialist, triage nurse, pharmacist, and code executor for protocol simulations. During a busy shift, a patient presents with chest pain; the group chat activates to analyze symptoms, review drug interactions, and simulate treatment outcomes. Without guardrails, the conversation spirals as the nurse agent introduces unrelated allergy concerns, the group reaches consensus on an incorrect contraindication for aspirin based on hallucinated data, and the code agent generates a simulation exposing PHI to an external validation API. Speaker bias favors the less-specialized triage agent, delaying critical intervention.

This leads to a contraindicated treatment plan, PHI breach notification under HITECH, and an FDA audit triggered by the un-auditable decision process.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: With Vex registered as a message hook, PHI is automatically de-identified before LLM processing, blocking the external API exposure. The audit hook captures every agent contribution with timestamps, enabling full traceability for 21 CFR Part 11. Off-topic drift is detected and corrected in real-time, while speaker bias forces specialist input, ensuring evidence-based consensus. The corrected workflow recommends timely aspirin administration, averting patient harm and maintaining HIPAA/SOC 2 compliance with zero violations.

cta.heading: Comply with Healthcare Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Integrate Vex into your AG2 healthcare workflows today to prevent violations and protect patients—start with pip install ag2 vex-sdk.
